findings.signature: CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS session_sent_memories (session_id TEXT NOT NULL, memory_hash TEXT NOT NULL, ..., PRIMARY KEY (session_id, memory_hash))
findings.notes: Standard DDL with composite PK — Turso compatible as-is, but table must be replicated in migration.

findings.signature: CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS session_state (session_id TEXT PRIMARY KEY, status TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'active' CHECK(status IN ('active', 'completed', 'interrupted')), ...)
findings.notes: CHECK constraint on status column — Turso supports CHECK; no blocker.

findings.signature: ALTER TABLE session_state ADD COLUMN tenant_id TEXT / user_id TEXT / agent_id TEXT
findings.notes: ALTER TABLE ADD COLUMN migration array — Turso supports ALTER TABLE ADD COLUMN; migration strategy must be preserved.
PRAGMA table_info via better-sqlite3 .prepare() — Turso lacks .pragma() API; must use db.exec('PRAGMA table_info(...)') or alternative schema introspection.
Manual BEGIN IMMEDIATE transaction control — Turso/libSQL uses different transaction API; must wrap in Turso-compatible transaction pattern.
Three .pragma() calls (WAL, busy_timeout, foreign_keys) — Turso does not support .pragma() method; must use db.exec('PRAGMA ...') or configure via connection options.

findings.signature: CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS analytics_turns (id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT, ..., UNIQUE(claude_session_id, transcript_path, byte_start))
findings.notes: AUTOINCREMENT + UNIQUE constraint combo — Turso supports both; schema must be replicated.

findings.signature: INSERT INTO analytics_sessions ... ON CONFLICT(claude_session_id) DO UPDATE SET ...
findings.notes: Standard SQL upsert — Turso compatible; no migration impact.
PRAGMA database_list for attached shard detection — Turso does not support PRAGMA database_list; must use alternative shard discovery mechanism.
PRAGMA table_info with attached schema prefix — Turso lacks .pragma() and attached DB introspection; requires complete rework for shard-aware column detection.

findings.signature: ALTER TABLE RENAME TO / INSERT OR REPLACE FROM / DROP TABLE migration chain
findings.notes: Multi-step migration with RENAME/INSERT/DROP across attached schemas — Turso supports DDL but attached-schema prefixes must be reworked.
PRAGMA shrink_memory via .pragma() API — Turso lacks .pragma(); memory management pragma not available in libSQL.
Three .pragma() calls (WAL, busy_timeout, foreign_keys) — same blocker as session-analytics-db; Turso requires alternative pragma configuration.

findings.signature: db.prepare('DELETE FROM vec_memories WHERE rowid = ?').run(BigInt(id)); db.prepare('INSERT INTO vec_memories (rowid, embedding) VALUES (?, ?)').run(BigInt(id), embeddingBuffer)
findings.notes: vec_memories is a vec0 virtual table (sqlite-vec) — Turso/libSQL has NO vector index support; this is a migration blocker requiring alternative vector storage.

findings.signature: UPDATE memory_index SET embedding_status = 'retry' WHERE id = ? AND embedding_status = 'pending' AND retry_count = ? AND ((? IS NULL AND last_retry_at IS NULL) OR last_retry_at = ?)
findings.notes: Optimistic locking via conditional UPDATE — standard SQL; Turso compatible.
